output.description: A liver-safe peach jam recipe.
output.directions:
1. To start, heat a water bath canner and sterilized pint jars for about ten minutes.
2. Run the lids through the boiling water for a few moments.
3. Set up a large bowl of ice water.
4. In a separate large pot boil water to blanch the peaches.
5. Place three peaches at a time in the water for about 20 seconds, then plunge in ice water.
6. When cool to touch the skins should remove easily.
7. Dice peaches finely and measure out 1 2/3 cups per batch.
8. Empty large pot of water used for blanching, and heat it on high with peaches, water, lemon juice and pectin.
9. Bring to a rolling boil, stirring constantly.
10. Add sugar and bring back to a boil for about 1 minute.
11. Ladle jam into jars using a funnel and leave 1/4 inch headspace (room between jam and the top of the jar).
12. Run a knife (or bubble removing tool) against the side of the jar to remove bubbles.
13. Wipe rims of the jars and place lids on.
14. Screw rims on just to finger tight.
15. Place jars into the waterbath.
16. Water level should be about 1-2 inches over the top of the jars (add additional water if necessary).
17. Once the water boils again, leave in for 10 minutes.
18. After ten minutes, turn off heat and let jars remain in hot water for another 5 minutes.
19. Then remove with jar lifter onto a kitchen towel.
20. Allow to cool.
21. When cooled you will notice the jar top will “pop” so that you cannot push it in and out.
22. This should happen in 24 hours.
23. If it does not, then refrigerate and use the jam right away.
24. Jar rims can be loosened slightly when stored (to prevent moisture from rusting the lid).

output.recipe_name: Nimo'S Chicken And Butternut Squash Stew
output.description: A liver-safe nimo's chicken and butternut squash stew recipe.

ingredients.name: 4-5 chicken thighs, skin removed and fat trimmed
mushrooms, sliced and given a rough chop
large handful green beans, trimmed, cut in thirds

output.directions:
1. Place chicken, carrot, onions, mushrooms, garlic and thyme sprigs in a large soup pot or Dutch oven.
2. Fill pot with cold water so that it covers top of the chicken and veggies.
3. Bring to a boil with salt, pepper and turmeric.
4. Lower heat to a simmer until chicken is tender and falls easily off the bone, about 1 to 1 1/2 hours.
5. Discard any fat you can skim off the top.
6. Take the chicken out and pull off the bone, shredding the chicken as you go.
7. Return the shredded chicken to the pot and discard bones and other waste.
8. Add butternut squash and bring back to a boil.
9. Cook on low until squash is falling apart, about 45 minutes.
10. Use the back of wooden spoon to break apart the squash so that it incorporates into the broth.
11. Add green beans (or spinach) and cook an additional 10 minutes.
12. Season with additional salt and pepper to taste.

output.description: A liver-safe yummy summer rolls recipe.
output.directions:
1. Place bean threads in a bowl and pour boiling water over it to cover.
2. Soak for 10-15 minutes until softened, then drain.
3. Use a wet kitchen towel or wet paper towels to assemble the rolls.
4. Keep another kettle of hot water ready to prepare your rice papers.
5. In a shallow pie plate pour hot water and the place rice paper 1-2 at a time in the water to soften for about 1 minute.
6. Once soft remove and place on your wet towel, flattening out the best you can (it will be like wet-tissue paper, so careful not to tear.
7. Place a stack of veggies that takes up about about 2 x 4 inches centered on the rice paper, including 1 -2 small lettuce leaves, 2-4 basil leaves, 2-4 mint leaves, small sprig of cilantro, and 1-2 tsp. shredded carrot.
8. Place a pinch of these the bean threads on top of your veggie pile.
9. Fold the top and bottom over the pile.
10. Then fold the right side over the pile and begin to roll the summer roll like a log until the left free edge covers up and seals the entire roll.
11. Cut in half before serving.
12. For best results eat soon after preparing, or store for a short time covered with a slightly damp paper towel.
13. Dip in your favorite sauce.

6. Remove tempeh from the marinade with a slotted spoon and cook in the stir-fry pan until slightly browned, about 3-4 minutes.
7. Add veggies and about half of the tempeh marinade.
8. At this point begin boiling your udon noodles in the pot for about 4 minutes or according to package instructions.
9. Continue to cook veggies for about 3-4 minutes.
10. Drain udon noodles and add to stir fry pan with the remaining marinade.
11. Cook an additional 2 minutes while stirring marinade into the noodles.
